editor.template_hint: Mark where each variable belongs with its name wrapped in double curly braces — for example, a variable named "genre" becomes genre wrapped in two opening and two closing braces.

metatags.title: Meta-tags
metatags.subtitle: Structural and style tags Suno recognizes inside lyrics.

settings.title: Settings

settings.lufs_target_hint: Used as the starting suggestion when exporting takes. Suno's default sits around -14 LUFS.

guide.title: Suno recording guide
guide.subtitle: How to capture voice and guitar takes that get the best results.
guide.intro: Suno performs best with clean, unambiguous references. This guide covers preparation, target levels, and a 3–5 minute recording script.

prep.title: Preparation
prep.body: Record in a treated room or one with plenty of soft material (curtains, rugs, clothes in a closet). Turn off fans and anything that hums. Warm up your voice for 5 minutes before recording.

levels.title: Levels: -12 to -6 dBFS
levels.body: Aim for peaks between -12 and -6 dBFS on the meter. Below that range, background noise becomes more noticeable; above it, clipping risk rises. Use the meter on the Record page to set your gain before you start.

distance.title: Mic distance: 15–30 cm
distance.body: Stay 15 to 30 cm from the microphone. Closer than that exaggerates plosives and proximity effect; farther away picks up more room reverb and noise.

script.title: Recording script
script.vowels: Sustained vowels — a, e, i, o, u
script.spoken_phrase: One spoken phrase, so Suno can read your vocal tone

expectations.title: What Suno expects
expectations.body: WAV, 48 kHz, mono, dry (no reverb or effects), 3 to 5 minutes of reference. The cleanest, most representative 2 minutes of the take usually produce the best results.

suno_voice_full.name: Full vocal script for Suno
suno_voice_full.desc: 5 guided minutes: warm-up, vowels, scales, two song excerpts, dynamics, and a spoken phrase.

step.warmup_inst: Loosen up with yawns, lip trills (brrr), and a soft hum for about a minute. No need to record — it's just to relax your throat before singing.
step.vowels_inst: Hold each vowel — a, e, i, o, u — for 3 to 4 seconds at your comfortable pitch, with a steady volume. This helps Suno pick up the pure timbre of your voice.
step.scales_inst: Sing a simple scale up and then down, on "la" or "na". It doesn't need to be perfect — the goal is to map your vocal range.
step.section_inst: Sing 8 to 16 bars of the song, with the real intention you want to convey. Prioritize clarity and presence over technical perfection.
step.section2_inst: Sing another 8 to 16 bars — ideally a chorus or a section that contrasts with the previous one. More variety helps the model learn your voice.
step.dynamics_inst: Sing the same phrase starting very soft and ending loud, without straining your throat. This shows Suno your voice's dynamic range.
step.spoken_inst: Speak — don't sing — a short, natural phrase, as if you were chatting. This helps Suno recognize the real timbre of your voice.

errors.device_busy: Audio device is in use by another program.
errors.already_recording: A recording is already in progress.
errors.ffmpeg_missing: FFmpeg isn't installed — install it to convert this file.
errors.permission_denied: Permission denied for the given path.
errors.insecure_context: Browser recording needs HTTPS (or localhost) — mic access is blocked on insecure connections.
errors.mic_denied: Microphone access was denied. Allow it in your browser settings and try again.
errors.empty_upload: The recording came out empty — try again.
